prompt: Which Greek god is the ruler of the sea?
In Norse mythology, what is the name of the great ash tree that connects the nine worlds?
Which Egyptian god has the head of a jackal and guides souls to the afterlife?
In Greek mythology, what creature has the body of a lion and the head of a human?
In Norse mythology, Odin sacrificed one of his eyes — but what did he get in return?
options: Wisdom from the well of Mimir

prompt: The Greek hero Achilles was invulnerable everywhere except one spot. What was his weakness?
In Japanese mythology, the sun goddess Amaterasu hid in a cave and plunged the world into darkness. What lured her out?
options: The sound of a rooster crowing

prompt: In Egyptian myth, the god Set killed Osiris and scattered his body into 14 pieces. Who reassembled him?
In Greek myth, Prometheus stole fire from the gods — but what was the punishment chained to a rock brought him daily?
options: Snakes bit his feet for eternity
His blood was slowly drained into the sea
He was forced to hold up the sky
A vulture ate his liver, which regrew each night

prompt: In Norse myth, the trickster Loki fathered many strange children. Which of these is NOT one of Loki's offspring?
The Aztec god Quetzalcoatl was a feathered serpent — but what was one of his key roles in Aztec cosmology?
options: God of war who demanded blood sacrifice
Ruler of the dead who judged souls
Creator of humanity using bones from the underworld

prompt: In Mesopotamian myth, the hero Gilgamesh sought a plant of immortality at the bottom of the sea. What ultimately happened to it?
options: He gave it to the gods in exchange for safe passage
A serpent stole it while he slept
He ate it but it turned him mortal anyway
It dissolved when he reached the surface

prompt: In Norse mythology, who is the god of thunder who wields a hammer named Mjolnir?
Which winged horse of Greek myth sprang from the blood of the slain Medusa?
In Greek myth, what was the name of the monstrous woman whose gaze turned people to stone?
According to Roman legend, which twin brothers were raised by a she-wolf and went on to found Rome?
In Greek mythology, who was the king cursed so that everything he touched turned to gold?
In Greek legend, how did the Greeks finally sneak their soldiers inside the walls of Troy?
options: By scaling the cliffs at night
Through a secret tunnel under the wall
Hidden inside a giant wooden horse

prompt: In Greek myth, what was the home of the gods, the tallest mountain in Greece?
In the Greek myth of Theseus, what did Ariadne give him so he could find his way back out of the Labyrinth?
In Greek mythology, why did the craftsman Daedalus warn his son Icarus not to fly too close to the sun?
options: The air was too thin to breathe
The sun god would strike him down
The wax holding his feathers would melt

prompt: In Hindu mythology, which elephant-headed god is revered as the remover of obstacles?
In Greek myth, the musician Orpheus journeyed to the underworld to retrieve his wife Eurydice. What condition caused him to lose her forever?
options: He failed to play his lyre
He looked back at her before reaching the surface

prompt: In Irish folklore, what is a leprechaun traditionally said to do for a living?
In Greek mythology, eating the seeds of which fruit bound Persephone to spend part of each year in the underworld?
In Norse mythology, what is the name of the rainbow bridge connecting the realm of the gods to the world of humans?
For one of his twelve labours, Heracles had to clean the vast Augean stables in a single day. How did he manage it?
options: He burned the filth away with fire
He commanded an army of birds to carry it off
He summoned a great wind to blow them clean
He diverted a river to wash them out

prompt: In Norse mythology, what was foretold to kill the god Baldur, despite his mother securing oaths from nearly everything not to harm him?
In the Welsh and Arthurian tradition, what is the name of King Arthur's legendary sword?
In Greek myth, the Sphinx of Thebes devoured travellers who failed her riddle. Who finally answered it correctly?
In Polynesian mythology, the demigod Maui is famous in many legends for accomplishing which feat?
options: Freezing the sea to cross it
Slowing the sun so days would be longer
Stealing thunder from the storm gods

prompt: A mythical bird said to burst into flames and be reborn from its own ashes is known as the what?
In Greek myth, a beautiful youth fell in love with his own reflection in a pool. What was his name?
Which Titan of Greek myth was condemned to hold up the sky on his shoulders for eternity?
In Greek myth, the many-headed Lernaean Hydra had a nasty trick. What happened when you cut off one of its heads?
options: Two heads grew back in its place
It shrank a little each time

prompt: A genie, or magical spirit, that grants wishes in Middle Eastern folklore is also known by what name?
In Greek myth, what was the name of the three-headed dog that guarded the entrance to the underworld?
Roman and Greek gods often mirror each other. The Greek god Zeus is known by which name in Roman mythology?
In Greek myth, King Sisyphus was punished in the underworld by being forced to do what for all eternity?
options: Be chained while a bird ate his liver
Pour water into a leaking jar
Roll a boulder uphill, only for it to roll back down
Reach for fruit that always pulled away

prompt: When Pandora opened the forbidden jar and released all the world's evils, what single thing was left inside?
In Norse mythology, what is the name of the prophesied final battle in which many of the gods are destined to die?
In Greek myth, the weaver Arachne challenged a goddess to a contest and was transformed into a spider. Which goddess did she offend?
In West African and Caribbean folklore, which trickster figure is usually depicted as a clever spider?
In Norse mythology, the warrior maidens who chose which slain fighters would be brought to Valhalla were called what?
In Jewish folklore, a golem is a creature made of clay or mud that is brought to life by what means?
In Japanese folklore, a kitsune is a magical fox said to grow extra tails with age and gain more power. How many tails does the most powerful kitsune have?
In Norse mythology, the goddess Idunn guarded apples that did what for the gods?
options: Granted them the power of prophecy

prompt: In Egyptian belief, the heart of the dead was weighed against a single feather to judge the soul. The feather belonged to which goddess of truth?
In Finnish mythology and the epic Kalevala, what is the Sampo, the mysterious object at the heart of the saga?
options: A harp strung with a maiden's hair
A boat that sails on its own
A magical mill that produces wealth and grain

prompt: In Hindu mythology, the gods and demons jointly churned the cosmic ocean of milk in order to obtain what prize?
options: A weapon to end all wars

prompt: In Babylonian creation myth, the storm god Marduk slays the primordial sea goddess Tiamat and forms the heavens and earth from what?
options: A cloud of stardust he gathered
Clay shaped by the other gods
